meta.title: Proof-Ingestion Decoupling — Parallel Task Breakdown (Tasks 1–27)
meta.scope: Tasks 1–22 cover the full PRD: A (Block Payload Refactor), B (Proof Pool & Ingestion), C (Replay-Free Apply Path), D (DA Decoupling), E (Owned-Object Certificate Fast Path), F (Scope-Aware Routing), G (Cross-Zone Messages & Forced Inclusion), H (Benchmark Harness). Tasks 23–27 (Workstream S: Safety, Hardening & Adversarial Testing) extend the plan beyond the PRD.
meta.how_to_use: 1) Pick a task whose blocked_by are all completed (status=completed). 2) Read its anchor files to understand current code. 3) Implement the build spec, satisfying EVERY acceptance criterion with tests. 4) Set status=completed. 5) If you changed a shared interface (the BlockPayload enum, header fields, apply seam), update the tasks listed in your 'blocks' array. Default mode FRACTAL_BLOCK_PAYLOAD_MODE=legacy MUST keep legacy blocks encoding byte-identically and tx_root deterministic.
global_invariants: Do NOT remove the current full-transaction block path (legacy mode stays).
Do NOT weaken proof verification into trusted-sequencer assertions (fail closed).
Unsupported production proof systems MUST fail closed; dev-digest mode only for local benchmarking.
Legacy block encoding and tx_root must remain byte-for-byte stable.

tasks.subject: Add BlockPayload variants (legacy vs proof-ingestion)

anchor.current_fields: Block carries transactions: Vec<Transaction>, eth_signed_raw: Vec<Option<Vec<u8>>>, da_sidecar: DaSidecar directly

tasks.build: New module crates/consensus/src/payload.rs introducing the shared payload contract (see shared_contract). Wrap the existing block body behind this enum so legacy full-tx blocks encode/decode byte-identically and tx_root stays deterministic. Add a versioned payload-root slot in the block header. Add an RpcBlock payload-type field.
tasks.shared_contract: pub enum BlockPayload { FullTransactions { transactions: Vec<Transaction>, eth_signed_raw: Vec<Option<Vec<u8>>> }, ProofUpdates(Vec<ZoneProofUpdateV1>), CertificateBatches(Vec<OwnedObjectCertificateBatchV1>), Mixed(Vec<BlockPayloadItem>) } | pub enum BlockPayloadItem { Transaction(Transaction), ProofUpdate(ZoneProofUpdateV1), CertificateBatch(OwnedObjectCertificateBatchV1) }
acceptance: Legacy full-transaction blocks still encode/decode unchanged.
tx_root deterministic for legacy blocks (regression test vs current).
New payload variants are committable in the header via a versioned payload root.
RPC reports the payload type for a block.
tasks.dependencies: none — foundational shared interface

tasks.subject: Add proof-update payload root
tasks.build: Deterministic Merkle/root commitment fn proof_updates_root(updates: &[ZoneProofUpdateV1]) in crates/consensus/src/payload.rs binding, per leaf: zone_id, parent_root, new_root, da_root, message_root, circuit_version, proof_digest. Implement the root fn standalone; wire into BlockPayload::ProofUpdates once A1 lands.
acceptance: Root is stable across nodes (pure function of ordered leaves).
Root binds zone_id, parent_root, new_root, da_root, message_root, circuit_version, proof_digest.
Header hash changes if any proof-update field changes (mutation test).
Unit tests: single, multi, empty, field-tamper cases.
tasks.dependencies: A1 (#1) for the enum variant

tasks.subject: Compatibility mode env flag (FRACTAL_BLOCK_PAYLOAD_MODE)
tasks.build: Read env FRACTAL_BLOCK_PAYLOAD_MODE=legacy|proof_ingestion|mixed at node init and gate block-proposal payload selection. Default legacy.
acceptance: legacy matches current behavior exactly.
proof_ingestion produces proof/certificate payloads when available, else empty.
mixed can include shared-state transactions plus proof updates.
Mode is logged at startup and queryable via RPC/health.
tasks.dependencies: none — gate added independently; payload producers (B/E) land separately

tasks.subject: Add ProofPool separate from tx mempool
tasks.build: New module crates/mempool/src/proof_pool.rs (or small crate): ProofPool storing pending ZoneProofUpdateV1 keyed by (zone_id, height). Conflicting same-key updates rejected or retained as evidence. Own metrics struct ProofPoolMetrics independent of MempoolLaneMetrics.
acceptance: ProofPool stores/evicts updates by (zone_id, height).
Conflicting same-key updates rejected or retained as slashable evidence (configurable).
Metrics (pending count, insert rate, conflict count) independent of tx mempool metrics.
tasks.dependencies: none — standalone pool + tests

tasks.subject: Add fractal_submitProofUpdate RPC (direct proof-update path)

anchor.current_submit: crates/node/src/lib.rs:1584-1613 (submit_proof_hash wraps ProofCommitmentV1 into mempool PooledTx)

tasks.build: New RPC fractal_submitProofUpdate that pushes a ZoneProofUpdateV1 directly into ProofPool (B1) so it is includable in a proof-ingestion block WITHOUT consuming transaction gas. Keep fractal_submitProofHash as a compatibility wrapper.
acceptance: New fractal_submitProofUpdate RPC method registered.
fractal_submitProofHash remains as compat wrapper / legacy method.
Direct proof submission includable in a proof-ingestion block without consuming tx gas.
Tests for both paths.
tasks.dependencies: B1 (#4 ProofPool), A1 (#1 payload variant)

tasks.subject: Verify proof public inputs against block payload
tasks.build: Verification routine binding a ZoneProofUpdateV1 proof's public inputs to the committed payload root (A2) and DA commitment. Implement the verifier fn standalone; wire once A2 lands.
acceptance: Verification FAILS if parent_state_root, state_root, tx_root, da_root, message_root, or circuit metadata mismatch committed values.
Unsupported production proof systems FAIL CLOSED.
Dev-digest proof mode available ONLY for local benchmarking (non-default flag), never on by default.
Unit tests: each field mismatch rejects; digest-mode rejected unless flag set.
tasks.dependencies: A2 (#2 payload root to bind against)

tasks.subject: Split block application into BlockApplyMode verification modes
tasks.build: New enum BlockApplyMode { ReplayFullTransactions, VerifyProofAndDa, HeaderOnlyAfterProofFinal } in a consensus/core module, plus dispatch skeleton in the apply path selecting mode by payload type. Introduce a trait/seam so C2 fills the verify branch. Keep all three paths callable.
tasks.shared_contract: pub enum BlockApplyMode { ReplayFullTransactions, VerifyProofAndDa, HeaderOnlyAfterProofFinal }
acceptance: Legacy blocks use replay mode (existing behavior).
Proof-ingestion blocks use VerifyProofAndDa.
Replay mode remains available for archival/full-verifier nodes.
Consensus validators can SKIP transaction replay for proof-covered zone updates.
Mode selection testable in isolation.
tasks.dependencies: none — enum + dispatch seam; verify-mode body filled by C2

tasks.subject: Update state roots from verified proof updates
tasks.build: Implement the VerifyProofAndDa branch of BlockApplyMode (C1): for proof-ingestion blocks, state transition comes from accepted proof updates (B3), NOT local tx execution.
acceptance: Zone state root advances ONLY if proof verification (B3) succeeds.
Global/masterchain root derives deterministically from accepted zone roots.
Rejected proof updates do NOT mutate state (rollback / no-op).
Tests: accepted update advances root; rejected update leaves state unchanged; deterministic masterchain derivation.
tasks.dependencies: C1 (#7 apply seam), B3 (#6 proof verifier)

tasks.subject: Add proof-finality indexing (per-zone/update)
tasks.build: Extend finality tracking from block-level to zone/UPDATE-level proof finality. New RPC e.g. fractal_getProofFinalHeight(zone_id). Persist a small index in storage so it survives restart.
acceptance: RPC exposes soft/proof finality for individual proof updates (per (zone_id, height)).
RPC can query latest proof-final height per zone.
Proof-finality survives restart once persistence is enabled.
No regression to existing block finality RPC.
tasks.dependencies: none — extends existing RPC/storage

tasks.subject: Replace transaction-list DA with zone-blob DA
tasks.build: Add a zone-blob DA path submitted INDEPENDENTLY of the base-chain tx list (not derived from borsh(transactions)). Header commits namespace, DA root, byte count, share count, sampling params. Proof finality requires DA verification. Legacy tx-list DA still works for legacy blocks. Implement zone-blob encoding/commitment standalone; wire header once A1 lands.
acceptance: Zone blob DA can be submitted independently of base-chain tx list.
Header commits namespace, da_root, byte count, share count, sampling params.
Proof finality (C3/B3) requires DA verification of the zone blob.
Legacy tx-list DA path still works for legacy blocks.
tasks.dependencies: A1 (#1 header commitment fields)

tasks.subject: Add DA sampling receipt / certificate
tasks.build: DaSamplingReceipt type + verifier so validators confirm availability WITHOUT reconstructing every payload. Self-contained (operates over commitments/indices).
acceptance: Sampling receipt binds sampled share indexes and the commitments sampled against.
DA verification can PASS using only the receipt (no full payload reconstruction).
Full reconstruction remains available for archive/debug nodes.
Tests: valid receipt verifies; tampered index/commitment rejects; insufficient samples rejects.
tasks.dependencies: none — receipt type + verifier self-contained

tasks.subject: Split DA fee accounting from execution gas
tasks.build: Split DA fee accounting from EVM/native execution gas. Separate fee/metrics channels. Fee-policy module exposing three cost categories: DA bytes, proof verify, shared-state execution.
acceptance: DA fee metrics reported separately from execution gas metrics.
Proof updates pay proof-verification cost separately (not execution gas).
Shared-state transactions continue using execution gas.
Fee-policy module exposes the three cost categories for the benchmark to read.
tasks.dependencies: none — accounting/metrics module, standalone + unit tests
files_likely_touched: new fee-policy module (e.g. crates/core or crates/consensus/src/fees.rs)

tasks.subject: Add owned-object certificate request + countersign RPC
tasks.build: Missing network/RPC path around existing cert types. Client requests owned-object precheck data; validators countersign eligible owned txs; client aggregates 2f+1 signatures into a certificate (uses existing aggregate).
acceptance: User/client can request owned-object precheck data (new RPC).
Validators countersign eligible owned transactions (network handler).
Client can aggregate 2f+1 signatures into a certificate.
Tests cover request -> countersign -> aggregate round trip.
tasks.dependencies: none — builds on existing cert crypto; new RPC + network handlers

tasks.subject: Add CertificatePool + direct owned-object finality
tasks.build: New CertificatePool that accepts valid certificates and gives them direct (object-local) finality without waiting for global tx ordering. Hook point for block cert-batch roots (root math is E3).
acceptance: CertificatePool accepts valid certificates (verify via existing verify).
Conflicting object versions rejected and exposed as slashable evidence.
RPC reports certificate finality for the object.
Blocks include certificate batch roots for history/proof inputs (hook point).
tasks.dependencies: none — standalone pool + tests using existing cert/evidence types

tasks.subject: Add certificate batch payload root
tasks.build: Deterministic batch root for OwnedObjectCertificateBatchV1 (the CertificateBatches payload variant from A1) so certs are committed in proof-ingestion blocks WITHOUT replaying the underlying transactions. Implement root fn standalone; wire once A1/E2 land.
acceptance: Batch root is deterministic (pure function of ordered certificates).
Batch root binds all certificate hashes AND object versions.
Conflicting certificates CANNOT appear in one accepted batch (validation rejects; test it).
Unit tests: empty, single, multi, duplicate-object-version rejection.
tasks.dependencies: A1 (#1 CertificateBatches variant), E2 (#14 CertificatePool supplies accepted batches)

tasks.subject: Route by execution scope, not only signer
tasks.build: Add scope-aware routing alongside existing signer-based home-shard routing. Owned Agent(agent_id) ops route by agent id; Owned Receipt(receipt_id) ops route by receipt id; wallet anchors route by commitment; Shared/EVM txs keep consensus routing. Introduce a deterministic route_key from TxExecutionScope + OwnedObjectId so owned objects land on a home shard independent of the signer address.
acceptance: Owned Agent(agent_id) operations route by agent id.
Owned Receipt(receipt_id) operations route by receipt id.
Wallet anchors route by commitment.
Shared/EVM transactions keep consensus routing.
Route key deterministic and unit-tested per scope.
tasks.dependencies: none - extends existing shard routing; soft link to E1/E2 for the owned-object fast path

tasks.subject: Add routing diagnostics
tasks.build: Expose routing diagnostics: RPC reports computed home shard and route key; mempool rejects wrong-shard submissions with detailed route info; load tests report per-shard imbalance. Build the diagnostics layer on existing signer routing now; add scope-key (F1) reporting once #16 lands.
acceptance: RPC reports computed home shard and route key for a submission.
Mempool rejects wrong-shard submissions with route details (source shard, expected shard, route key).
Load tests can report per-shard imbalance.
No behavior change to acceptance/rejection logic - observability only.
tasks.dependencies: none - diagnostics layer; scope-key reporting layers on F1 (#16) when it lands

tasks.subject: Add message root payloads
tasks.build: Make cross-zone interactions asynchronous via message roots. Zone proof update includes an outbound message root (merkle over queued AsyncCrossZoneMessageV1); base chain orders/commits message roots (via the versioned payload root from A1); destination zone consumes messages by CrossZoneMessageInclusionProofV1. Wire the existing message_root field already bound by A2 proof_updates_root.
acceptance: Zone proof update includes outbound message root.
Base chain orders/commits message roots.
Destination zone consumes messages by inclusion proof.
Tests: round-trip submit -> root -> order -> consume.
tasks.dependencies: A1 (#1) for base-chain payload commitment; message_root field already bound by A2 (#2)

tasks.subject: Add forced-inclusion queue root
tasks.build: Censorship escape hatch. Base chain accepts forced-inclusion requests and commits a forced_inclusion_queue_root (settlement-envelope field via A1). Zone proof MUST include required forced-inclusion items after their deadline_masterchain_height elapses; missing items reject zone proof finality (hook into B3/C3).
acceptance: Base chain accepts forced-inclusion requests.
Base chain commits forced_inclusion_queue_root.
Zone proof must include required forced-inclusion items after timeout.
Missing forced-inclusion items reject zone proof finality.
Tests: timeout forces inclusion; inclusion satisfied -> finality ok.
tasks.completion_notes: Added deterministic forced_inclusion_queue_root commitments to MasterchainBlockV1, registry/ledger queue-root helpers, per-zone due forced-inclusion root enforcement during proof-final updates, and tests for pending/due queue commitments, missing timed-out inclusion rejection, and satisfied inclusion finality. Verified with cargo test -p fractal-shard forced_inclusion --lib, cargo test -p fractal-shard --lib, and cargo check --workspace.
tasks.dependencies: A1 (#1) for base-chain queue-root commitment; finality-rejection hooks into B3 (#6) / C3 (#9)

tasks.subject: Baseline benchmark path (current fractalchain, full tx execution)

anchor.bench_lib: crates/benchmarks/src/lib.rs (535-614 cert throughput, 616-659 DA sampling, 661-739 proof latency, 741 mixed SLO)
anchor.load_tps: tools/load-tps/src/main.rs:1-269 (chain TPS, submit TPS, block rate, tx/block)

tasks.build: Ensure the benchmark harness runs the BASELINE (current fractalchain, full transaction execution) across all H1 scenarios and emits the required metric set. Scenarios: native NoOp load; owned-object tx load; proof commitment load; mixed EVM/native load; BFT-7 validator lab. Metrics: submitted tx/sec, committed tx/sec, block p50/p95 production latency, CPU & memory, block bytes, DA bytes, validator replay time. Emit a stable JSON summary so H3 can diff against the proof-ingestion run.
acceptance: All five baseline scenarios runnable with a single command.
Metrics captured: submitted/committed tx/sec, block p50/p95 latency, CPU/mem, block bytes, DA bytes, replay time.
Emits a JSON summary file for the baseline run.
BFT-7 validator lab scenario included.
tasks.completion_notes: Added a one-command fractal-baseline-bench binary and stable BaselineBenchReport JSON schema covering native NoOp, owned-object tx, proof commitment, mixed EVM/native, and BFT-7 validator lab scenarios. Each scenario records submitted/committed tx/sec, block p50/p95 latency, cpuNanos, peakWorkingSetBytes, block/DA bytes, replay time, and BFT quorum metrics where applicable. Verified with cargo test -p fractal-bench and a JSON output smoke run.
tasks.dependencies: none - benchmarks the existing baseline path; independent of proof-ingestion work

tasks.subject: Proof-ingestion benchmark path (fractalchain2)
tasks.build: Benchmark fractalchain2 with proof-ingestion payloads. Scenarios: accepted proof updates/sec; accepted certificate updates/sec; mixed proof updates + shared-state txs; DA sampling enabled; BFT-7 validator lab. Metrics: accepted proof updates/sec, accepted certificate updates/sec, block p50/p95 production latency, proof verification time, DA sampling time, CPU/mem, payload bytes. Emits a JSON summary in the same shape as H1 for diffing in H3.
acceptance: Proof-ingestion scenarios runnable (proof updates/sec, cert updates/sec, mixed, DA-sampling, BFT-7).
Metrics captured: accepted proof/cert updates/sec, block p50/p95, proof verify time, DA sampling time, CPU/mem, payload bytes.
Emits a JSON summary with the same schema as the baseline (H1).
Comparison shows whether proof ingestion raises throughput/lowers latency without weakening quorum safety.
tasks.completion_notes: Added run_proof_ingestion_bench and a one-command fractal-proof-ingestion-bench binary using the same BaselineBenchReport schema as H1. Scenarios cover proof updates/sec, certificate updates/sec, mixed proof updates plus shared-state tx payloads, DA-sampling proof updates, and BFT-7 proof-ingestion quorum formation. Metrics include accepted proof/cert update rates, block p50/p95, proof verify time, DA sampling time, cpuNanos, peakWorkingSetBytes, payload bytes, replay time, and BFT quorum counts. Verified with cargo test -p fractal-bench and a JSON output smoke run.
tasks.dependencies: B2 (#5 submitProofUpdate), C2 (#8 state roots from proof updates), E3 (#15 cert batch payload) - the proof-ingestion path must exist end-to-end to benchmark it; DA-sampling scenario layers on D2 (#11)

tasks.subject: Comparison report (baseline vs proof-ingestion)
tasks.build: Script that ingests the baseline (H1) and proof-ingestion (H2) JSON summaries and emits a comparison report: per-metric deltas, plus a bottleneck-category classification per scenario (consensus, proof verification, DA sampling, network, or storage). Human-readable output (markdown/HTML) generated from the two JSON files.
acceptance: Script emits/reads JSON summaries for both repos.
Report includes baseline vs proof-ingestion deltas for every metric.
Report classifies the dominant bottleneck category per scenario.
Human-readable report generated from the two JSON files.
tasks.dependencies: H1 (#20 baseline summary) and H2 (#21 proof-ingestion summary)
tasks.completion_notes: Added scripts/compare-proof-ingestion-bench.py. The script reads H1 baseline and H2 proof-ingestion JSON summaries, canonicalizes comparable scenario names, computes per-metric deltas, classifies bottlenecks across consensus/proof verification/DA sampling/network/storage, and emits JSON, Markdown, and HTML reports. Verified with small fractal-baseline-bench and fractal-proof-ingestion-bench runs plus generated comparison JSON/Markdown/HTML.

tasks.subject: Make DevDigest impossible in production builds/configs
tasks.build: Combine compile-time and runtime hard-gating so a production build cannot construct or accept a DevDigest proof. Gate the DevDigest variant + its verify arm behind a Cargo feature 'dev-digest' that is OFF by default and excluded from release/mainnet profiles. Add a runtime guard that hard-rejects DevDigest at BOTH submission and verification when a production/release profile or FRACTAL_NETWORK=mainnet-style flag is active. Remove any ambient default that could enable it.
acceptance: A release/non-dev build physically cannot construct or verify a DevDigest proof (compile-time where possible, else runtime hard-reject).
DevDigest remains usable ONLY under the explicit dev-digest feature + local benchmark configs.
A test asserts that under production config a DevDigest proof is rejected at submission AND verification.
No regression to benchmark scenarios that legitimately use DevDigest (they run under the dev feature).
tasks.dependencies: B3 (#6, proof verifier where the DevDigest arm lives)
tasks.completion_notes: Gated ValidityProofSystem::DevDigest behind the non-default fractal-consensus/dev-digest feature, preserved explicit Borsh discriminants so default builds reject DevDigest tag 0 instead of mapping it to production proofs, added a production/mainnet runtime guard for opt-in dev builds, mapped disabled dev proofs to a node rejection reason, moved proof-latency benchmarks to production fixture proofs, and updated node proof-finality tests to use production proof fixtures. Verified with cargo test -p fractal-consensus --lib, cargo test -p fractal-consensus --features dev-digest --lib dev_digest, cargo test -p fractal-node --test apply_synced_rpc_maps, cargo test -p fractal-bench, and cargo check --workspace.

tasks.subject: Adversarial tests for proof public-input mismatches across every root
tasks.build: Adversarial test suite that, for EVERY committed root (proof-update root #2, certificate-batch root #15, message root #18, DA root #10, forced-inclusion queue root #19, legacy tx_root), mutates each bound public-input field and asserts verification FAILS. Cover field swap, single-bit flip, truncation, stale-root replay, and cross-root confusion (root A inputs under root B). Fields: parent_state_root, state_root, tx_root, da_root, message_root, circuit_version/metadata, proof_digest, public_input_digest.
acceptance: One test per (root x field) mismatch; every case rejects.
Cross-root confusion cases reject.
Tampered / stale / timestamp-mismatch cases reject (extend the 3398 pattern).
Suite runs in CI; no input mutation slips through verification.
tasks.completion_notes: Added adversarial tests for proof-update roots, certificate-batch roots, consensus block validity public inputs, shard zone proof public-input digests, message/DA/forced-inclusion cross-root confusion, stale forced-inclusion replay, and zone-proof commitment proof_digest/prover tampering. Gated DevDigest-only tests behind the dev-digest feature so default CI remains green while the DevDigest adversarial suite runs with --features dev-digest. Verified with cargo test -p fractal-consensus --lib, cargo test -p fractal-consensus --features dev-digest --lib, cargo test -p fractal-shard --lib, cargo check --workspace, cargo fmt --all --check.
tasks.dependencies: B3 (#6 verifier) + all roots: #2(done), #10, #15, #18, #19

tasks.subject: Property/fuzz tests for payload root ordering and mutation resistance
tasks.build: proptest + cargo-fuzz targets over every payload-root function asserting ordering sensitivity (any non-identity permutation yields a different root), mutation resistance (single-bit flip in any leaf yields a different root), determinism (identical inputs -> identical root), ordered/non-commutative behavior where required, empty/single-leaf boundaries, and no trivial collisions over small input spaces. Add targets under fuzz/fuzz_targets/.
acceptance: proptest/cargo-fuzz targets added for each root fn.
All properties hold over generated input distributions; failing shrinks reproduce the bug.
Property tests run in CI; fuzz targets documented for nightly/periodic runs.
tasks.completion_notes: Added proptest coverage for proof-update roots, certificate-batch roots, full/mixed payload roots, DA commitment hashes, ordering sensitivity, single-bit mutation resistance, determinism, and small-space collision checks. Added cargo-fuzz targets for payload roots and DA commitment/header roots under fuzz/fuzz_targets/. Verified with cargo test -p fractal-consensus --lib payload --no-fail-fast, cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins, and cargo fmt --all --check.

tasks.subject: Stress forced-inclusion liveness (multi-zone, delayed proofs)
tasks.build: Integration/stress harness with multiple execution zones and delayed/withheld proofs asserting BOTH liveness (after deadline_masterchain_height a forced-inclusion request is eventually included; report worst-case inclusion latency in blocks) and safety (a zone proof omitting a DUE forced-inclusion item is rejected, finality withheld, until included). Stress dimensions: zone count, proof-delay distribution, adversarial sequencer withholding, concurrent forced requests.
acceptance: Multi-zone liveness: forced requests included within a bounded number of blocks after deadline.
Delayed-proof: missing forced items reject zone-proof finality until included.
Adversarial sequencer withholding eventually overridden by the forced-inclusion path.
Latency/throughput reported in the benchmark metric style.
tasks.completion_notes: Added a multi-zone forced-inclusion stress harness in the dedicated masterchain tests. The scenario creates four zones with staggered deadlines, submits eight concurrent forced-inclusion requests, withholds proofs until all deadlines have elapsed, asserts base-chain queue roots stay committed, reports worst_inclusion_latency_blocks, rejects omitted due roots, and then accepts satisfied forced-inclusion roots for each zone. Verified with cargo test -p fractal-masterchain and cargo check --workspace.
tasks.dependencies: G2 (#19 forced-inclusion root) + C2 (#8 state-root apply / finality); soft link to C3 (#9)

tasks.subject: Define minimum DA sampling parameters, reject undersampled receipts
tasks.build: Replace ad-hoc/parameter-passed minimums with a single DaSamplingPolicy source of truth, justified by soundness: min sample count as a function of blob share count + Reed-Solomon expansion ratio + target collision probability; min columns/rows; confidence threshold. Enforce the policy at BOTH the receipt-verify boundary (strengthen 1451-1463) AND the commitment-build boundary (1648). Eliminate magic-number literals (e.g. min_samples: 4) in callers/tests - reference the policy.
acceptance: A single DaSamplingPolicy module/source of truth; all callers reference it instead of literals.
Receipts below the policy minimum are rejected at BOTH build and verify boundaries.
Policy parameters documented with their soundness rationale (sample count vs blob size).
Tests: receipt at min-1 rejects; receipt at min+ passes; required sample count scales with share count.
tasks.dependencies: D2 (#11 receipt, done) + D1 (#10 zone-blob DA share structure)
